title: The World Around Us Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Putting seeds or young plants into the soil so they can grow.
rounds.answer: planting
rounds.explanation: planting: Putting seeds or young plants into the soil so they can grow.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The main part of a plant that holds up the leaves.
rounds.answer: stem
rounds.explanation: stem: The main part of a plant that holds up the leaves.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The brown earth on the ground where plants grow.
rounds.answer: soil
rounds.explanation: soil: The brown earth on the ground where plants grow.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
rounds.answer: watering

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The colourful part of a plant that makes new seeds.
rounds.answer: flower

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The planet that we all live on; it is our home.
rounds.answer: earth

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Planting trees helps clean the air and gives us shade.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Please do not drop litter; use a bin instead.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Water travels up the stem to reach the leaves.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: What does the flower of a plant make?
rounds.answer: New seeds
rounds.explanation: A flower makes the seeds that grow into new plants. Soil, rain and sunlight are not made by any flower.

rounds.prompt: According to “Plants and Clean Air”, which gas do plants give out?
rounds.answer: Oxygen
rounds.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide and give out oxygen, which is the gas that we breathe.

rounds.prompt: Look at the picture: Nora ______ the flowers right now.
rounds.answer: is watering
rounds.explanation: Nora is one person, so she takes is, and the verb keeps its -ing ending.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What is Mother Earth Day?
choices: It is a special day when people all around the world show that they care about our planet.
It keeps the Earth clean and safe for animals.
A lot of nice fresh air.
rounds.answer: It is a special day when people all around the world show that they care about our planet.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: It is a special day when people all around the world show that they care about our planet.

rounds.prompt: What is Sami’s father doing, and who is helping him?
choices: It keeps the Earth clean and safe for animals.
They found litter: old bags and papers lying in the soil.
He is planting trees, and his uncle is helping him.
rounds.answer: He is planting trees, and his uncle is helping him.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: He is planting trees, and his uncle is helping him.

rounds.prompt: What did the children do with the paper they picked up, and why is that good for the Earth?
choices: They found litter: old bags and papers lying in the soil.
They put it in a box for recycling. Nora explains that when we recycle paper, fewer trees are cut down.
Their green leaves clean the dirty air, so the air we breathe stays good.
rounds.answer: They put it in a box for recycling. Nora explains that when we recycle paper, fewer trees are cut down.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: They put it in a box for recycling. Nora explains that when we recycle paper, fewer trees are cut down.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Talk About Environmental Actions
Speaking 3 — Say Thank You to Nature
Speaking 5 — Earth-Care Reporter

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A bird built its nest high in the tall tree.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The seeds grew well in the soft, dark soil.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Adam felt happy when the first green stem appeared.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'flower' mean in this unit?
choices: The colourful part of a plant that makes new seeds.
Putting seeds or young plants into the soil so they can grow.
Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
rounds.answer: The colourful part of a plant that makes new seeds.
rounds.explanation: flower: The colourful part of a plant that makes new seeds.

rounds.prompt: What does 'glad' mean in this unit?
choices: Putting seeds or young plants into the soil so they can grow.
Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
Feeling happy and pleased about something good that has happened.
rounds.answer: Feeling happy and pleased about something good that has happened.
rounds.explanation: glad: Feeling happy and pleased about something good that has happened.

rounds.prompt: What does 'watering' mean in this unit?
choices: Rubbish that people drop on the ground in public places.
Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
Putting seeds or young plants into the soil so they can grow.
rounds.answer: Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.
rounds.explanation: watering: Giving water to plants so that they do not dry out.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'air'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: air is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'appreciate'?
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: appreciate is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'roots'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: roots is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: Lifting things off the ground and taking them away.
Turning used things, like paper or glass, into something new.
The main part of a plant that holds up the leaves.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The colourful part of a plant that makes new seeds.
A tall plant with a wooden trunk, branches and leaves.
The brown earth on the ground where plants grow.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Feeling happy and pleased about something good that has happened.
Feeling glad for something good and wanting to say thank you.
Mattering a lot, so that we must care about it.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Amal put the tiny ______ into the soil, and months later they grew into trees.
rounds.answer: seeds
rounds.explanation: Seeds are the tiny plant parts that grow into new plants. Buttons, shoes and roads never grow.

rounds.prompt: Amal is holding two small seeds in her hand. Which sentence is correct?
choices: This seeds in my hand are small.
These seeds in my hand are small.
Those seeds in my hand are small.
That seeds in my hand are small.
rounds.answer: These seeds in my hand are small.
rounds.explanation: The seeds are near her hand and there is more than one of them, so the pointing word is these.

rounds.prompt: Which of these actions keeps our environment clean and safe for animals?
choices: Dropping a bottle on the path
Leaving the tap running all day
Picking up litter in the park
rounds.answer: Picking up litter in the park
rounds.explanation: Picking up litter keeps parks and streets safe for animals. The other three choices leave harm behind them.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word means rubbish that people drop on the ground in public places?
rounds.answer: litter
rounds.explanation: Litter is dropped rubbish. Sunshine is light from the sun, oxygen is a gas, and a branch is part of a tree.

rounds.prompt: What does recycling do?
choices: It waters a plant so that it grows.
It counts the birds in a garden.
It measures how tall a tree is.
It turns used things into something new.
rounds.answer: It turns used things into something new.
rounds.explanation: Recycling makes used paper, glass or plastic into something new to use again. The other three describe completely different jobs.

rounds.prompt: Which of these makes food for a plant using sunlight?
rounds.answer: The green leaves
rounds.explanation: Leaves use sunlight to make the plant's food. Soil, air and rain are around the plant, but they are not parts of it.
